zh.lifeSummary: 當Gilchrist Mure of Rowallan and Polkelly於1269年出生在英國蘇格蘭艾斯爾時，他的父親Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallan38歲，母親Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallan34歲。他和Isabel de Comyn至少有1個女兒。他去世於1297年在英國蘇格蘭享年28歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gilchrist Mure of Rowallan and Polkelly于1269年出生在英国苏格兰艾斯爾时，他的父亲Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallan38岁，母亲Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallan34岁。他和Isabel de Comyn至少有1个女儿。他去世于1297年在英国苏格兰享年28岁。

nameMeanings.name: Mure
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Italian (Sicily): variant of   Morea  .
Scottish: variant of   Muir  .
English: occupational name from Old French muer ‘mewer’, i.e. someone who tended to hawks in the cages in which they were kept while moulting. Compare Mew .

variants.name: Mura
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Akio, Hideo, Masao, Nanako, Tsutomu, Yoshi.  French Alphonse, Amie, Henri.
German: altered form of some similar (like-sounding) German or perhaps French surname. It is also common in France (Alsace), where it was first recorded in the form Morrau, later Murra(w).
Italian (southern Sardinia): topographic name or nickname from Sardinian mura ‘mulberry’ or ‘blackberry’.

variants.name: Muir
meanings: Scottish: topographic name for someone who lived on a moor, from Older Scots muir ‘moor’ (an expanse of bare, wild upland country). There has been some confusion with Mure .

variants.name: Morea
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Saverio, Domenic, Ferdinando, Rocco.  Spanish Manuel, Carlos, Cesar, Elvira, Jesus, Mauricio.

Italian (Apulia): from the personal name Morea, originally a habitational name from Morea, the medieval name of the Greek region of Peloponnese, named as the land of mulberry trees, from mōrea ‘mulberry’.
Spanish and Catalan: habitational name from any of the places called Morea, in Badajoz, Navarre, and Lleida provinces.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Isidor, Myer.

English, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a maker of hoops and bands, etc., from Middle English band, bond, Middle High German, Middle Low German bant, German Band ‘hoop, metal band, fetter, shackle’, i.e. something used for tying or binding.
English: variant of   Bond  .

variants.name: Bandt
meanings: German: variant of   Band  .

variants.name: Bandstra
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Wouter.
West Frisian: habitational name composed with Band 4 + the suffix -stra from Old Frisian sittera ‘inhabitant of’.

variants.name: Banta
meanings: West Frisian: probably a habitational name for someone from Bant, in the 17th century an island in Friesland, now a village north of Emmeloord in the Noordoostpolder (see   Band   4, compare   Banda   5). This surname is no longer found in the Netherlands.
History: Epke Jacobs Banta arrived in New Netherland in 1659.

variants.name: Bandel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Zvi, Emanuel, Hyman, Meir.
South German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from a diminutive of   Band  . See also   Bendel  .
German: in some cases, a variant of Pandel, a pet form of the medieval personal name Pantaleon (see Pantaleo ).

variants.name: Bend
meanings: English (Leicestershire and Nottinghamshire): from Middle English bend ‘band, fetter, chain; ribbon, sash, stripe’, for someone who made, wore, or used such items. See also the Band 1 and compare Bendy 1.

variants.name: Bandler
meanings: German: occupational name for a maker of band (for vessels) or ribbon or lace, from an agent derivative of Middle High German bandel, a diminutive of bant ‘band’ (see Band ).

variants.name: Bandy
meanings: English: perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English bandi, bondi, bendi, derivatives of Middle English and Old English band, bond, bend ‘band, ribbon, stripe’ + Middle English -i, reinforced by Old French bendé ‘striped’, with reference perhaps to clothing ornamented with ribbons or stripes, or to the diagonal band of a heraldic device. Compare Band .
English: alternatively, perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English bandy, bendy ‘curved stick’, used for someone with bent or crooked legs.
Czech and Slovak: from a derivative of banda ‘(gipsy) band’, apparently a nickname for a bandsman. This surname is found among Gypsies.

variants.name: Bond
meanings: English: status name for a peasant farmer or husbandman, Middle English bond(e), bounde, occasionally bande ‘bondman, customary tenant, serf’ (Old English bonda, bunda, reinforced by Old Norse bóndi). The Old Norse word was also in use as a personal name (Old Norse Bóndi, Bondi, Bundi, Bonde, borrowed as late Old English Bonda), and this has given rise to other English and Scandinavian surnames alongside those originating as status names, such as the Middle English personal name Bonde. The status of the peasant farmer fluctuated considerably during the Middle Ages; moreover, the underlying ancient Germanic word is of disputed origin and meaning. Among ancient Germanic peoples who settled to an agricultural life, the term came to signify a farmer holding lands from, and bound by loyalty to, a lord; from this developed the sense of a free landholder as opposed to a serf. In England after the Norman Conquest the word sank in status and became associated with the notion of bound servitude. The name can also be a variant of Band .
Swedish: variant of   Bonde  .
In some cases also an American shortened form of Ukrainian Bondarenko and possibly also of some other surname beginning with Bond-.

nameMeanings.name: Gilchrist
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (western) and Irish (Down): from Gaelic Mac Giolla Chríosd, Mac Gille Chríosd ‘son of the servant of Christ’.

variants.name: Kilcrease
meanings: Altered form of Scottish   Gilchrist  .
History: Robert Kilcrease (c. 1750–1808) came from Scotland(probably Kilmarnock) to Edgefield, SC, c. 1770.

variants.name: Gilchrest
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Gilchrist  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Gilkerson
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Chrìosd ‘son of Gille Chrìosd' (see Gilchrist ).

variants.name: Gilkey
meanings: Irish (northern): possibly a pet form of the Scottishpersonal name   Gilchrist  .

variants.name: Gilcrest
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Gilchrist  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Gilcrease
meanings: Probably an altered form of Scottish   Gilchrist  .

en.lifeSummary: When Gilchrist Mure of Rowallan and Polkelly was born in 1269, in Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallan, was 38 and his mother, Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallan, was 34. He had at least 1 daughter with Isabel de Comyn. He died in 1297, in Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 28.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gilchrist Mure of Rowallan and Polkelly est né en 1269, à Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallan, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallan, avait 34 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Isabel de Comyn. Il est décédé en 1297, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 28 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gilchrist Mure of Rowallan and Polkelly 1269 in Ayrshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallan 38 und seine Mutter Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallan 34. Er hatte mit Isabel de Comyn mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1297 im Alter von 28 Jahren in Schottland, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gilchrist Mure of Rowallan and Polkelly è nato il nel 1269 nel Ayrshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallan, aveva 38 anni e il suo madre, Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallan, aveva 34 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Isabel de Comyn. Morì nel 1297, nel Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 28 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Gilchrist Mure of Rowallan and Polkellyが1269年にイギリススコットランドエイルシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallanは38歳，母Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallanは34歳であった。彼はIsabel de Comynとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1297年にイギリススコットランドで28歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Gilchrist Mure of Rowallan and Polkelly(이)가 1269년 영국 스코틀랜드 에이르실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallan(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallan(은)는 34세였다. 그는 Isabel de Comyn(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1297년 영국 스코틀랜드에서 28세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gilchrist Mure of Rowallan and Polkelly nasceu em 1269, em Ayrshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallan, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallan, tinha 34 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Isabel de Comyn. Ele faleceu em 1297, em Escócia, United Kingdom, com 28 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gilchrist Mure of Rowallan and Polkelly родился в 1269 г. в местности аиршир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallan было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallan, 34. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Isabel de Comyn. Он умер в 1297 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 28 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Gilchrist Mure of Rowallan and Polkelly nació en 1269, en Ayrshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallan, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallan, tenía 34 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Isabel de Comyn. Murió en 1297, en Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 28 años.
